seoTitle: Hay South vs Haymarket — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Hay South and Haymarket. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Hay South vs Haymarket: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hay South and Haymarket.
heading: Hay South vs Haymarket — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hay South, New South Wales and Haymarket, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Haymarket has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Hay South (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hay South or Haymarket safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hay South has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Haymarket scores 22/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hay South scores 23/100 for bushfire risk. Haymarket scores 27/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hay South or Haymarket?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Haymarket has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.18% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
